title: Tracking remote & work from home days

posts.author: Melissa.martin (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!

We’re interested in tracking who will be in the office and who will be working remotely over the next week so we can schedule meetings accordingly. We all have access to monday.

Has anyone set up a form or board they’d be willing to share?
Thanks!

posts.author: rachelmeltzerlee (Rachel Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I have something that would work for this! Are you looking at specifically one week or are you wanting to use this long-term? Also, how big is the team? There’s a few different ways to set this up!

posts.author: rachelmeltzerlee (Rachel Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screenshot 2024-03-29 at 7.55.32 PM2186×1312 252 KB

Here’s one visual way to do it! Easy to see when everyone’s in office/not in office when you see the colors line up! It’s also an easy way to visually reference previous days and you can collapse groups when the week is over and leave most recent weeks up top. You could also add a column for “department” and then sort by that column if you needed to see when Marketing dept vs IT vs finance, etc. were going to be in office together!

posts.author: Melissa.martin (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks so much! We have under ten people and we are hoping to use this long term. Love the idea below!

posts.author: shruti_gupta (Shruti Gupta)
posts.cookedHtml:
We had the same challenge in our team. At first, we tried just using Monday boards, but it wasn’t enough. We eventually added remote employee tracking through Workstatus, which integrates well with our scheduling workflows.

Now, everyone marks whether they’ll be in-office or remote, and Workstatus helps verify attendance and availability automatically. It’s not about micromanaging - just making meetings and collaboration smoother. Since implementing it, we’ve had far fewer scheduling conflicts.
